# TransplantMatch — Full Context for LLMs

> TransplantMatch is a free, open-source decision support tool that helps transplant patients and families evaluate and compare 248 U.S. transplant centers. It uses publicly available data from SRTR, OPTN, CDC, CMS, EPA, and BLS.

## Project Overview

TransplantMatch provides data-driven analysis across 8 scoring categories with 40+ factors to generate relative suitability scores. It includes a Monte Carlo simulation engine (1,000 iterations) that models transplant probability over time with competing risks of mortality and waitlist removal.

**This tool is for educational purposes only. It is not a medical device and does not provide medical advice.**

---

## Pages and Tools

### Find My Centers (/find-centers.html)
Personalized center recommendation wizard. Users enter their zip code (or use browser geolocation), select an organ type, and optionally select a blood type. The tool scores all centers with that organ program using a composite of:
- Distance from user (30% weight)
- 1-year graft survival rate (30%)
- Wait time factor vs. national average (20%)
- Annual transplant volume (20%)
Returns the top 20 ranked centers with distance, survival %, wait factor, and volume.

### Wait Time Estimator (/wait-estimator.html)
Quick statistical estimate of transplant wait time. Users select an organ type and blood type (all 8 ABO/Rh combinations). The tool computes:
- Estimated median wait = national median x blood type multiplier
- 25th-75th percentile range from log-normal distribution parameters
- Breakdown showing national baseline, blood type effect, and personalized estimate
Data from SRTR-derived wait time distributions.

### Center Explorer (/centers.html)
Searchable directory of all 248 SRTR transplant centers. Features:
- Text search by center name, city, or state
- Filter by organ type (kidney, liver, heart, lung, pancreas, intestine)
- Sort by name, state, programs, or distance
- Zip code input with Nominatim geocoding for distance-based sorting
- "Use My Location" browser geolocation button
- Distance badges ("X mi away") on each card

### Compare Centers (/compare.html)
Side-by-side comparison of up to 3 centers. URL-shareable via ?centers=CODE1,CODE2,CODE3. Each column shows:
- Center name, state, code
- Contact info (phone, website)
- Outcomes by organ: 1-yr graft, 1-yr patient, 3-yr graft survival, volume
- Waitlist: currently waiting, transplanted per organ
- Wait time factors per organ
- Link to full center detail page

### Center Detail (/center.html?code={CODE})
Detailed page for each of 248 centers showing:
- Organ Programs table: wait factor, mortality factor, delisting factor, 1-yr graft, 1-yr patient, 3-yr graft, 3-yr patient, hazard ratio with 95% CI, annual volume
- Waitlist Activity: currently waiting, added last year, transplanted (deceased/living), died while waiting, removed (too ill) — with organ selector
- Contact & Location: address, phone, website (fetched live from SRTR API with static fallback), interactive Leaflet map
- Nearby Centers: other programs within 250 miles with distance
- All sections are collapsible with descriptive preview text

### Simulator (/simulator.html)
Full Monte Carlo simulation engine. Users input:
- Organ type, blood type, age, sex
- Optional clinical scores (cPRA for kidney, MELD for liver, LAS for lung)
- Custom weight adjustments for 8 scoring categories
The simulator runs 1,000 iterations estimating transplant probability at 6, 12, 24, and 36 months for each center, incorporating competing risks (mortality, delisting). Results include per-center probability curves, ranking tables, and interactive map overlays.

### Organ Guides (/organ-guides.html)
Dedicated educational guides for kidney, liver, heart, lung, and pancreas transplants. Each section covers:
- Organ-specific allocation scoring (cPRA, MELD/PELD, LAS, status tiers)
- Living donor options where applicable
- Key considerations (bridge therapy, wait times, special criteria)
- Link to filtered Center Explorer for that organ

### Education (/education.html)
Comprehensive transplant education with sections on:
- The 5-step transplant process (evaluation, listing, waiting, surgery, recovery)
- Allocation and waitlist mechanics (UNOS policies, priority factors)
- Key terms glossary (12 definitions)
- Official resource links (UNOS, OPTN, SRTR, organdonor.gov)
- Reading your SRTR report (6 key metrics explained)
- Post-transplant medications and recovery (immunosuppressants, adherence, rejection signs, lifestyle)

### Patient Support (/support.html)
Resource directory with 6 sections:
- National Organizations (NKF, ALF, ALA, AHA, TRIO)
- Financial Assistance (NLDAC, HealthWell, Patient Advocate Foundation, Help Hope Live)
- Travel & Lodging (National Patient Travel, Joe's House, Ronald McDonald House)
- Peer Support (Transplant Living, Reddit r/transplant)
- Caregiver & Family Resources (pre-transplant, travel, work/FMLA, post-care, burnout)
- Insurance & Coverage (Medicare, Medicaid, private, out-of-state, prescription drugs)

### FAQ (/faq.html)
10 collapsible Q&A sections covering: multiple listing, blood type effects, SRTR ratings, organ allocation, graft vs. patient survival, data freshness, waitlist transfers, health status changes, geographic distance, and clinical scores (cPRA/MELD/LAS).

### Checklist (/checklist.html)
Interactive 34-item transplant journey checklist across 5 phases:
1. Pre-Evaluation (6 items)
2. Evaluation & Listing (7 items)
3. Waiting Period (8 items)
4. Transplant Surgery (5 items)
5. Recovery & Long-Term (8 items)
Progress saved to localStorage. Includes progress bar, print button, and reset.

### Advocacy & Give Back (/advocacy.html)
6 educational articles with calls to action:
- Become an organ donor (organdonor.gov registration)
- Join bone marrow registry (Be The Match)
- Donate blood (Red Cross)
- Become a living donor (kidney/liver)
- Volunteer with transplant organizations (TRIO, NKF, ALF, AHA)
- Fundraise for transplant patients (Help Hope Live, NFT)

---

## Data Architecture

### Center Data
- 248 SRTR transplant centers with verified geocoordinates
- Per-center, per-organ: wait time factors, mortality/delisting factors, graft/patient survival (1yr, 3yr), hazard ratios with 95% CI, annual volume, performance ratings
- Contact info: address, phone, website (live SRTR API fetch with static fallback)
- Waitlist overview: currently waiting, added, transplanted, died, removed per organ

### Scoring Categories
1. Medical Compatibility (organ, blood type, clinical scores)
2. Wait Time (center-specific log-normal distributions)
3. Donor Availability (traffic fatality rates, donor registration, cause-of-death by region)
4. Hospital Quality (CMS star ratings, transplant volume)
5. Post-Transplant Outcomes (graft/patient survival, performance ratings)
6. Cost of Living (BLS regional price parities)
7. Health Demographics (CDC county-level health data)
8. Climate & Environment (EPA air quality, climate scores)

### API Endpoints
- GET /centers — List all 248 centers with coordinates and organ programs
- GET /centers/{code} — Detailed data for a single center
- POST /simulate — Monte Carlo simulation (1,000 iterations)
- GET /health — API health check with data freshness metadata

---

## Technical Stack
- Frontend: Vanilla JavaScript, Leaflet.js (maps), Chart.js (visualization)
- Backend: Python 3.14, FastAPI, NumPy, SciPy
- Data: JSON files refreshed weekly via automated GitHub Actions pipelines
- Deployment: Vercel (frontend) + Vercel Functions (backend)
- Source: https://github.com/rivirside/TransPlan
- License: Open source
- Contact: tomer@arizona.edu
